question.label: Senior forums
question.name: In senior forums, what makes you feel most unprepared?
question.description: Drills into the confidence thread to surface the specific gap underneath his hesitation.

answer: I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.

assessment.note: He names a specific self-limiting pattern — not being explicit about desired opportunities — which signals mild growth and engagement drag; this also hints at a clarity issue off-signature.

new_queue.label: Wanted opportunities
new_queue.name: What opportunities have you not been explicit about wanting?

new_queue.label: Direction
new_queue.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?

new_queue.label: Next-level meaning
new_queue.name: What does senior scope mean to you in practice?

new_queue.label: Ownership boundary
new_queue.name: What are you still carrying personally that should live with the team?

new_queue.label: First growth move
new_queue.name: What is the first shift you want to make before we meet again?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 5)

history.answer_excerpt: Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
